State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest

(916) 322-5660          322-5601          322-6461          322-6444

July 21, 1978

Terry Dixon
Office of Orange County
Counsel
P.O. Box 1379
Santa Ana, CA  92702

Dear Terry:

I reviewed the Fair Political Practices Commission's
advice files after our telephone conversation yesterday
afternoon. I found no letter of advice addressing the question of whether a candidate who raises $500 has to file a
Statement of Organization.

However, the staff has consistently advised that
a candidate does not have to file a Statement of Organization
when the candidate receives $500 or more. The candidate
will have to file candidate's campaign statements disclosing
all contributions received and expenditures made. Government
Code Sections 84200, 84201 and/or 84206.

If you have any further questions, please contact
me.

Sincerely

Barbara Campbell
Counsel
Legal Division

BC:plh

CALIFORNIA
GASP
GROUP AGAINST SMOKING POLLUTION

ADVISORY BOARD
Jesse L. Steinfeld, M.D.
U.S. Surgeon General, 1969-73
Dean, School of Medicine,
Medical College of Virginia

Linus Pauling
Nobel Laureate
Research Professor
Linus Pauling Institute
of Science and Medicine

Dr. Glenn T. Seaborg
Nobel Laureate
University Professor of Chemistry
University of California, Berkeley

J. William Futrell
President, Sierra Club

John H. Knowles, M.D.
Dean, School of Medicine
University of California, San Diego

Wilbert S. Aronow, M.D.
Professor of Medicine
Chief, Cardiology Division
Department of Medicine
University of California, Irvine

MAIN OFFICE
P.O. Box 1061
Berkeley, CA 94701
(415) 849-1744

SOUTHERN
CALIFORNIA
OFFICE
P.O. Box 45893
Los Angeles, CA 90045
(213) 671-1492

May 21, 1978

Mr. Alan Herndon
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Herndon:

Following our most recent conversation, I spoke to Mr. William Durley
in the Secretary of State's office and explained GASP's problem with
respect to our filing status. He assured me that if we filed as a
recipient committee retroactively, we would in all likelihood receive
a waiver. I have decided to file as a recipient committee as of
September 1, 1977, since I believe this is the "neatest" solution and
the one which will avoid any future problems.

I am enclosing a copy of my letter to Mr. Durley outlining the reasons
why we feel a waiver is appropriate, including the details of our
correspondence and telephone conversations. I trust that I have
accurately reflected the chain of events.

Thank you for your attention. I hope this matter can now finally
be laid to rest!

Sincerely,

[signature]

Peter Hanauer
Treasurer

Encl.
PH/ht

Paul L. Loveday          Timothy I. Moder, Ph.D.          Frank Wiswell          Peter Hanauer          Sandra Young
President                Executive Director               Southern California Coordinator    Treasurer             Newsletter Editor

CALIFORNIA
GASP
GROUP AGAINST SMOKING POLLUTION

ADVISORY BOARD

James L Steinfeld, M.D.
U.S. Surgeon General (1969-73)
Dean, School of Medicine,
Medical College of Virginia

Linus Pauling
Nobel Laureate
Research Professor,
Linus Pauling Institute
of Science and Medicine

Dr. Glenn T. Seaborg
Nobel Laureate
Professor of Physics,
University of California, Berkeley

J. William Futrell
President, Sierra Club

John H. Morley III, M.D.
Dean, School of Medicine
University of California, San Diego

Ibert S. Aronow, M.D.
Professor of Medicine
Professor of Community &
Environmental Medicine
University of California, Irvine

Pharmacology & Therapeutics
University of California, Irvine

MAIN OFFICE

P.O. Box 1061
Berkeley, CA 94704
(415) 849-1794

SOUTHERN
CALIFORNIA
OFFICE

P.O. Box 43893
Los Angeles, CA 90043
(213) 671-1492

May 21, 1978

Mr. William W. Burley
Office of the Secretary of State
Political Reform Division
111 Capitol Mall, Room 200
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

Dear Mr. Burley:

As per our phone conversations on May 19, I am enclosing a Statement
of Organization (Form 410) for California Group Against Smoking
Pollution (GASP) and an amended Campaign Statement for the filing
period ending on March 10. The amendment affects the change in our
status from an Expenditure Committee filing Form 461 to a Recipient
Committee filing Form 420. Our change in status is for the sole
purpose of complying with 2 Cal Adm Code Section 18215(d), an administrative regulation of the Fair Political Practices Commission, as it
pertains to dues and other contributions to membership organizations,
and in no way reflects any activities by GASP to raise funds directly
for the Clean Indoor Air Act Initiative.

We have decided to change our status and file these forms after lengthy
discussion and correspondence with Mr. Alan Herndon of the Fair Political
Practices Commission regarding the applicability of the above-mentioned
regulation to GASP. We feel very strongly that any fine that would
normally be levied against us due to late filing should be waived for
the following reasons:

1) As a result of our initial discussion with persons at the FPFC
in the spring of 1977, it was our impression that with the formation
of the Clean Indoor Air Committee (now the Campaign for Clean Indoor
Air) to be the recipient committee during our campaign, GASP would
be an expenditure committee only, and that it would be proper for
GASP to mention the Initiative as one of its activities as long as
we did not ask people to contribute to GASP in direct support of the
Initiative.

2) During the entire campaign, we have consistently made the
distinction to both our members and the general public between GASP
as an ongoing membership organization and the Clean Indoor Air
Committee as the organization directing the initiative campaign and
to which initiative contributions should be given.

Paul I Loveday          Timothy I Moder, Ph.D.          Frank Wagnell          Peter Hanauer          Sandra Yousos

Mr. William N. Durley                                          page two

3) After filing our first campaign statement on March 12, our
status was questioned by the Los Angeles County Registrar's Office,
which subsequently contacted the FPPC. Mr. Herndon in turn
contacted us, and it was only then that we became aware of the
FPPC regulation in question.

4) Further discussions with Mr. Herndon led to a letter from him
on April 20, indicating that the FPPC had determined that we were
not a recipient committee.

5) Within a few days thereafter, I contacted Mr. Herndon to inform
him that contrary to one of the assumptions made in his letter, CASF
had mentioned the Clean Indoor Air Act in letters to members asking
them to renew their memberships (but not in our basic brochure used
to attract new members). I then sent copies of proposed renewal
letters which I had hoped would obviate any problem with respect to
our status while still mentioning the Initiative.

6) Mr. Herndon informed me on May 15 that even these proposed
letters would not prevent us from being a recipient Committee.
Rather than continue to attempt to split hairs over the precise
language of our letters, we feel it will be better for all concerned
to change our status, and to do so retroactively so as to be sure to
cover all periods during which the letters in question have been used.

7) We have at all times attempted to comply with the requirements
of the Political Reform Act and to cooperate with the governing
agencies. We do not feel that we should be penalized because of a
misunderstanding resulting from being unaware of a regulation,
particularly in light of our good faith efforts to clarify the
situation upon learning of the misunderstanding.

Although not directly related to the waiver issue, I might point out
that in a further attempt to alert our members to the distinction
between CASF and the Campaign for Clean Indoor Air, the CASF Board
of Directors adopted a resolution at its regular meeting [in May]
to the effect that all funds raised between now and [illegible]
for memberships, shall go to the Campaign for Clean Indoor Air.

At your request, I am also enclosing the renewal letter which we
recently used for about six months, and which was the first to
mention the Initiative. Because we use the same letter over a long
period of time, we do not date them, and I therefore cannot pinpoint
the precise date on which we began to use it. I do know it was
in the late summer of 1977, and thus I am choosing September 1977
as the date on which CASF became a recipient committee under
of Form 440.

Mr. William N. Durley                                page three


     I hope that this letter and the enclosed forms will satisfactorily
clarify our situation. I would appreciate a response as to our
request for a waiver at your earliest convenience. Of course, if
you have further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

     Thank you for your attention.

     Sincerely,

     [signature]
     Peter Hanauer
     Treasurer

     Encl.

     cc:  Mr. Alan Herndon
          Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County
          Registrar of Voters, City and County of San Francisco

     PH:ht
